what_it_does: Defines a design-system preview card that renders selected CV icons at multiple sizes across bronze, gold, ink, and cream-on-dark tones.
observations: Includes @dsCard metadata for group, name, subtitle, and viewport.
Loads shared card styling from _card.css and icon data from ../assets/icons/cv-icons.js.
Uses CSS custom properties for typography, colors, radius, and backgrounds.
Defines five icon sizes: 16, 20, 24, 32, and 40 pixels.
Renders five icon names per tone: house, file, chat, dashboard, and network.
The lead text names bronze, gold, ink, muted, and cream tones, while the rendered tone map includes bronze, gold, ink, and cream only.
standouts: The preview combines tone and size demonstration in a compact grid.
Cream icons are shown on a dark stage to demonstrate contrast context.
The note states default tone, default size, and fixed stroke width.
conventions: Uses CSS variables rather than hard-coded design values for theme colors and fonts.
Keeps the preview self-contained with inline file-local CSS and JavaScript.
Constructs SVG examples from shared icon path data.
Uses uppercase small labels for row names.
concerns: The documented muted tone is not rendered by the TONES map or any row.
The script assumes window.CV_ICONS.data contains every listed icon name.
Using stage.innerHTML += inside the loop repeatedly reparses the stage contents.
notes: This file is a compact visual reference for CV icon tone and size variants.
questions: Should muted be rendered alongside the other documented tones?
Is the cream tone intended only for dark backgrounds?
Are the five listed icons representative examples or a fixed preview set?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose mostly matches: it demonstrates icon tones and sizes, but the file text includes muted while the rendered preview does not.
intention_signals: It seems intended to show designers and implementers how CV icons look when tone and size inputs are varied.
novelty: The file uses shared icon path bodies to generate multiple inline SVG previews instead of hard-coding each SVG instance.
contribution: Contributes a file-local reference for icon tone names, size examples, default icon settings, and dark-background cream rendering that a fused system would keep as preview and documentation material.
summary_for_embedding: HTML preview card that renders shared CV icon SVGs in several sizes and tone colors using design-system tokens.
intention_for_embedding: Created to document and visually demonstrate the icon tone and size options for handoff.
